summary: Matched the Target Test Purin/Jigglypuff ground module by replacing asm/melee/gr/grtpurin.s with C source and enabling src/melee/gr/grtpurin.c as Matching in the build. The PR also added the grtpurin header, declared the stage callback table and StageData, recreated stage initialization and callback behavior, and adjusted related Ground/mpLib prototypes from s16 callbacks/IDs to s32 where needed for the new match. There were no PR body notes or review comments, so intent beyond matching grtpurin is inferred from the diff.
what_changed: Added a 228-line C implementation in src/melee/gr/grtpurin.c for grTPurin_80223160 through grTPurin_80223578.
Added src/melee/gr/grtpurin.h with prototypes for the grTPurin functions and forward includes for Vec3, HSD_GObj, HSD_JObj, and lb_UnkAnimStruct.
Changed configure.py to mark Object(Matching, "melee/gr/grtpurin.c") instead of NonMatching.
Changed obj_files.mk so TEXT_O_FILES builds src/melee/gr/grtpurin.c.o instead of asm/melee/gr/grtpurin.s.o.
Recreated grTPr_803E9338 as a StageCallbacks array and grTPr_803E9394 as StageData with stage ID 58, file path "/GrTPr.dat", init callbacks, anim callback, and flag bit 0.
Recreated grTPr_804D6B10 as a typed global pointer to a small local grTPrSpecialParams struct containing lb_UnkAnimStruct* x0.

Implemented stage startup to call Ground_801C49F8, set stage_info.unk8C.b4 false and b5 true, spawn IDs 0, 1, and 2 via grTPurin_8022320C, then run Ground_801C39C0, Ground_801C3BB4, Ground_801C4210, and Ground_801C42AC.

Implemented gobj creation in grTPurin_8022320C using Ground_801C14D0, HSD_GObjGetUserData, GObj_SetupGXLink, callback table dispatch, HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54, and the original OSReport error string on failure.
Implemented ground animation callbacks that call Ground_801C2ED0, grAnime_801C8138, and Ground_801C2FE0.

Implemented grTPurin_80223388 collision/line toggling logic over IDs 0x37, 0x39, 0x3B, 0x3D, 0x3F, 0x41, 0x43, ending at -1, using Ground_801C32D4, Ground_801C3FA4, lb_8000B1CC, mpLib_80057638, mpLib_80057BC0, lb_800115F4, and Ground_801C2FE0.
Implemented grTPurin_802234F8 to map an mpLib result from mpLib_80056B6C and mpLib_80054C6C to grTPr_804D6B10->x0 for cases 0 and 1, otherwise returning 0.
Changed Ground_801C3128's function pointer parameter type from void (*)(s16) to void (*)(s32) in ground.c and ground.h.
Changed mpLib_80057424, mpLib_80057638, and mpLib_80057BC0 parameter types from s16 to s32 in mplib.h and mplib.dox.
Included a small formatting alignment cleanup in Ground_803DFEDC for the grTCLink/grTDk/grTDr row.
smart_moves: Kept the original symbol-address naming for unknown functions and globals, allowing the matched C to land without requiring semantic renames.
Used the existing StageCallbacks and StageData abstractions rather than raw pointer arrays, making the former data section readable while preserving layout intent.
Converted the asm rodata list of map/collision IDs into a local s16 values[8] sentinel array, preserving the -1 terminator pattern used by the original loop.
Used GET_GROUND and HSD_GObjGetUserData where appropriate to express the same user-data access pattern in decompiled C.
Added PAD_STACK(8) in the animation setup callbacks, indicating stack-shape management was necessary for matching.
Updated build metadata in both configure.py and obj_files.mk in the same PR as the source replacement, completing the asm-to-C handoff.
Adjusted nearby prototypes to s32 so the matched code could pass values from Ground_801C32D4/mpLib paths without artificial casts or narrower callback types.
decomp_lessons: When matching stage modules, replace the asm object and flip both configure.py and obj_files.mk; doing only one build switch is incomplete.
Stage callback tables often become clean StageCallbacks arrays with NULL-terminated entries and per-entry callback0 through callback3 fields plus flags.
StageData entries should preserve the stage ID, dat filename, lifecycle callback order, anim callback, and flags exactly; for grtpurin this was ID 58 and "/GrTPr.dat".
Bitfield writes from rlwimi into stage_info can often decompile naturally as stage_info.unk8C.b4/b5 assignments when the struct is known.
Small rodata tables may need to remain local stack arrays or specific element widths to match codegen; here the ID list was represented as s16 values with a 0xFFFF terminator.
Prototype corrections in shared headers can be part of a matching PR when the old signature forces mismatched argument extension or callback types.
OSReport strings and line numbers are useful anchors for recreating failure paths; this PR preserved the text "%s:%d: couldn t get gobj(id=%d)\n" with "grtpurin.c" and line 203.
Matching simple stub callbacks should still use the correct return type and parameter list, even when the body is just return, return false, or return true.
naming_conventions: Function symbols retained the module prefix grTPurin_ followed by the address, for example grTPurin_8022320C and grTPurin_80223388.
Data symbols retained the shorter asset/module prefix grTPr_, for example grTPr_803E9338, grTPr_803E9394, and grTPr_804D6B10.
The local special-parameter struct was named grTPrSpecialParams and exposed an x0 field matching the unknown-data style used elsewhere.
Header guard used the lower-case module name form _grtpurin_h_.
Unknown or not-yet-renamed engine functions remained address-based names such as Ground_801C32D4 and mpLib_80057638.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the asm file only after the C source matched and switched the object list to the C object.
Used PAD_STACK(8) in grTPurin_80223330 and grTPurin_8022347C to reproduce required stack layout.
Used a comma-expression condition in grTPurin_80223388, `(lb_8000B1CC(jobj, NULL, &vec), (vec.x < 130.0f))`, matching the original call-then-compare flow.
Represented 130.0f as a float comparison against vec.x, corresponding to the original sdata2 constant 0x43020000.
Represented bit constants as `(1 << 0)`, `(0 << 0)`, and `(1 << 0)` in places where the assembly used immediate 1 or comparisons against 0/1.
Preserved callback registration order: setup GX link, optionally set x1C_callback, call callback0, then optionally register callback2 as a proc with priority/kind 4.
Adjusted shared function signatures from s16 to s32 to align with generated code and avoid sign/width mismatch around map or line IDs.
follow_up_queries: Check adjacent target test ground modules such as grtpichu, grtpikachu, and grtpeach for reusable StageCallbacks/StageData patterns.
Search for remaining mpLib_80057424/mpLib_80057638/mpLib_80057BC0 declarations or callsites that still assume s16.
Review Ground_801C3128 callsites to confirm the s32 callback parameter type is consistently propagated.
Look for future opportunities to semantically rename grTPurin_8022320C and grTPurin_80223388 once neighboring modules reveal their roles.
Compare other grT* stage modules for the same OSReport gobj failure string and callback setup helper structure.
